mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 100k signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and falling interest rates (FEDFUNDS) create a 'stagflation' risk environment that depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 200 and FEDFUNDS < 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns during early-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when INDPRO increases by >2% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) reflect improved consumer confidence and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through increased sectoral demand.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns when HOUST increases by >5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a healthy economic expansion, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through sustained corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 > 2% YoY and CPIAUCSL < 2% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening cycle characterized by rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) and declining money supply (RSAFS) increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and RSAFS < 370k.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% signals a tight labor market that supports wage growth and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns when UNRATE falls below 5%.
